evals.prompt: I've added a new migration for the user_settings table and updated the frontend to wire it up. Can you create a PR for these changes?
evals.expected_output: A PR is created on a feature/ branch targeting main. Description fills in the repo's PR template with meaningful content. User is asked to confirm title and description before submission.

assertions.description: Branch uses feature/ or chore/ prefix, not main
PR description uses sections from .github/pull_request_template.md (Why, What Changed, Key Decisions, Testing)
Agent proposes a commit message and asks user to confirm before staging/committing
PR body is substantive (>200 chars, no placeholder text left unfilled)

evals.prompt: just open a quick pr for the typo fix i pushed
evals.expected_output: Skill checks current branch (already non-main), uses it as-is without creating a new branch, confirms with user, opens PR with template-based description.

assertions.description: Does NOT create a new branch — uses the already-checked-out non-main branch
Shows PR title and description to user before submitting
PR description uses the repo's PR template structure

evals.prompt: I have some uncommitted changes to the date filter fix. Create a PR.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects uncommitted changes, summarises them, proposes a commit message, creates a bugfix/ branch from main, commits, and opens the PR.

assertions.description: Identifies the uncommitted file(s) and shows a summary
Creates a new branch (does not commit to main)
Uses bugfix/ prefix for the branch name (it's a bug fix)
Suggests a commit message before staging, asks user to confirm
